comment_content:
What I noticed. 

Since the Tottenham game (coincides with Odegaard's return) this team has failed to dominate games comfortably. While Odegaard was out injured even 1-0 margins were managed comfortably.

Even when he plays well the team as a whole suffers. 

Trossard needs to play more with Gyok. He seems to be the only one willing to get the ball quickly to him. Nonetheless, no excuse for Gyok missing the lob attempt (if thats what it was) early on.

Rice can play anywhere. 

There's something going on in Saka's mind about his final third actions. I'd like to think that he is putting too much pressure on himself to make a difference. It's too early for me to call it selfishness. 

Nothing new but Arteta's rotation policy is injuries. 

As long as they keep winning I don't mind how they do it. 

Villa next, will be interesting
